item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed this! -2 and -3 are pretty interesting and are sufficiently explored in the test logs. The REDACTED detail about -3 bugged me a bit, but I guess it's not really important since all we need to know about them is that they're plants.

Regardless, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anomalous books are cool, generally. Anomalous systems of knowledge are really cool. Anomalous engineering feats have the potential to be cool. This just didn't do much for me. The logs went on too long without building to much of anything, and unless there's a specific reason that the researchers should be discovering something that could covered in a line in the description, it shouldn't be included as an addendum.

A potentially compelling idea executed poorly. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small heads up you used amnesiacs instead of amnestics in the first paragraph of the containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with the other people. This article needs cleanup. However, the concept is great, and the main things that need cleanup are the exaggerations (O5 council approval and such) and the stuff that the foundation wouldn't do, such as testing on civilians.

However, I still give this a +1.

One last thing: I think a test log of this scip would be great. There is a vast range of possibilities. I'm sure some people won't agree with me, but I think it's a good idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your very first test log specifically includes a guy with a PhD with no previous exposure to 3669, then the Foundation exposes him to 3669.

Granted I didn't have a problem with this (headcanon: protocol was much more lax in 1996!) but others do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The overall concept is interesting, but there are a lot of test logs, no indication of what happened during the breach, and the writing feels unedited. You've got a sentence that just stops after a word, for instance. Needs cleanup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to everyone for the suggestions. The idea behind the people being tested that were not D-Class were that they were previous SCP-3669-1s that were originally detained for testing prior to Incident 3669-47. I do not think that went through clearly so I will do some quick edits and fix some of the small errors in the formatting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I generally like mathematical skips and this was entertaining and well done. The test logs were a tiny bit dragged out… you probably could've removed one or two of them in the middle there. (Do we really need to know it works on Children? Or see it out in the wild?)

My only other negative comment is either the ending or the containment procedures need to be tweaked. Narratively, it was a little bit of a letdown. I'm not saying it wasn't imaginative and neat, it was, and I liked it. But given the absurdly overwhelming Containment Procedures (O5 approval, 50 km and 20 km perimeters, and requiring amnestics of EVERYONE in that huge area) I thought the ending was going to have a lot more oomph, and have a lot wider impact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: I'm not a mod, but you're not allowed to vote on your own article. I advise you to rescind your vote before a mod notices.

This was true in the past, but it is no longer true.

http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1861065/policy-update:self-upvoting

Upvoting your own article: You can upvote or even downvote your article as you like. Keep in mind that this used to be against the rules, so you may get negative comments from other users who are working off of outdated information. But it's up to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The foundation typically does not test on anything but D-class, this article seems to imply citizens used for testing; also the text itself could use some cleaning up.

her skin began exhibiting a green discoloration of the skin

is redundant for example
Other than that you got the tone right and the concept itself is unique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is like a reality-bending system of mathematics? An interesting idea, if not a little overdone.
However, there are many errors throughout your scip, mostly tonal errors.

Here's a quick few I picked up on-

+ show block
– hide block

every sapient person that entered

"sapient" seems rather redundant here

SCP-3669 is a book teaching the reader

You just stated that it was a book at the beginning of the previous paragraph

more simply

should be substituted for something such as "more effectively/efficiently"

based around arrows pointing in 1 of 8 directions

I'm not really sure what this means, and this in turn led the testing logs (and the entire SCP, really) to be rather confusing. More elaboration on how the system works would be nice.

SCP-3669-2 exclusively uses arrows until the solution, which is rendered numerically.

This sentence needs to be worded better. I suggest something along the lines of "SCP-3669-2 uses arrows exclusively to reach a numerical solution."

Also, there's a lot missing from your description that's covered in your logs. I recommend you cover more of the anomalous effects, primarily those outlined in your incident log and the effects and significance of SCP-3669-3.

Please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

-1 for now, until it's cleaned up and explained further.

Edit: I'm not a mod, but you're not allowed to vote on your own article. I advise you to rescind your vote before a mod notices. WHoops yeah I'm old
